cooked:
So, another round of correcting. For the texts, Im already at them and will be finished sooner or later. The language is much better than in LBA1. This time Ive got another problem - with the voices.

(Ive translated the quotes to English for your better reference)
On Emerald Moon, at a certain place, Baldino should say (as also in the text):

The doors jammed. We have to find some way to open it.

But instead you only hear:

The doors jammed.

When crash-landing on Otringal, he should say:

Twinsen, I damaged the vessel during landing. I need some time for repairs. The Zeelichian fuel is called gazogem, its very powerful. You should go get some.

But instead it goes like this:

We have to find some way to open it. (pause) Twinsen, I damaged the vessel during landing. I need some time for repairs. The Zeelichian fuel is called gazogem, its very powerful. You should go get some.

I dont know how the quote from Emerald Moon slipped over to Otringal, and I dont know how to fix it. I am a totally noob at sound programs and dont even have proper ones.
The translation guides are not helpfull this time, because I only have 2 (or maybe some more) entries too fix.

There are a few other problems:
After saving Raph, when with Zoe, if you keep walking into the wrong direction, Zoe should say (again this is the right way in the displayed text):

No, not through there. First Lets go over to the lighthouse and visit the Weather Wizard.

But instead she just says:

No, not through there.

When winning the race on Desert Island, the guy should say:

I am going to tell you a little secret: a wizard told me that he might have hidden a protective spell on the island across from the Hacienda.

But instead theres just a

I am going to tell you a little secret:

Ive never heard the other parts of the quotes somewhere, but they may be hidden somewhere in the .vox files at one of the quotes you never hear.

Now, I dont know how to fix this. I am sure one of the guys from the modifications forum can help me. Do you know how to do this?
- be sure youve read my whole post before responding. Thank you -
raw:
So, another round of correcting. :) For the texts, I'm already at them and will be finished sooner or later. The language is [i]much[/i] better than in LBA1. This time I've got another problem - with the voices.

(I've translated the quotes to English for your better reference)
On Emerald Moon, at a certain place, Baldino [i]should [/i]say (as also in the text):
[quote]
The door's jammed. We have to find some way to open it.
[/quote]
But instead you only hear:
[quote]
The door's jammed.
[/quote]
When crash-landing on Otringal, he [i]should[/i] say:
[quote]
Twinsen, I damaged the vessel during landing. I need some time for repairs. The Zeelichian fuel is called gazogem, it's very powerful. You should go get some.
[/quote]
But instead it goes like this:
[quote]
We have to find some way to open it. (pause) Twinsen, I damaged the vessel during landing. I need some time for repairs. The Zeelichian fuel is called gazogem, it's very powerful. You should go get some.
[/quote]

I don't know how the quote from Emerald Moon slipped over to Otringal, and I don't know how to fix it. I am a totally noob at sound programs and don't even have proper ones.
The translation guides are not helpfull this time, because I only have 2 (or maybe some more) entries too fix.

There are a few other problems:
After saving Raph, when with Zoe, if you keep walking into the wrong direction, Zoe should say (again this is the right way in the displayed text):
[quote]
No, not through there. First Let's go over to the lighthouse and visit the Weather Wizard.
[/quote]
But instead she just says:
[quote]
No, not through there.
[/quote]
When winning the race on Desert Island, the guy should say:
[quote]
I am going to tell you a little secret: a wizard told me that he might have hidden a protective spell on the island across from the Hacienda.
[/quote]
But instead there's just a
[quote]
I am going to tell you a little secret:
[/quote]
I've never heard the other parts of the quotes somewhere, but they may be hidden somewhere in the .vox files at one of the quotes you never hear.

Now, I don't know how to fix this. I am sure one of the guys from the modifications forum can help me. Do you know how to do this?
\- be sure you've read my whole post before responding. Thank you ;) -

cooked:
Sorry for the misunderstanding but I thought it was misplaced inside the HQR and not inside a WAV file.

For the sound specification you must follow this:
Type: ACM Waveform - Microsoft ACM: IMA ADPCM
Audio format: PCM (uncompressed)
Channels: Mono
Sample rate: 22 050 Hz
Bit resolution: 16 bit

Adobe Audition, is a sound edition program very good that its simple to use and could made what you need. Anyway, it isnt freeware software, you must pay for it, but if you want to download, well I cant stop you that
Try torrents, emule-project or something. You should get there.

cooked:
- Kobold: A torrent is some kind of information of the computer with BitTorrnet software from which the files are. This information is then handled by a BitTorrent client, which uses it to download the files in a torrent.

As for eMule - its P2P (peer-to-peer) file sharing client, just like eDonkey2000 and Shareaza (Shareaza also supports BitTorrent).

And yes, if you edit the files, you have to set the sound specification again before saving.
raw:
\- Kobold: A torrent is some kind of information of the computer with BitTorrnet software from which the files are. This information is then handled by a BitTorrent client, which uses it to download the files in a torrent.

As for eMule - it's P2P (peer-to-peer) file sharing client, just like eDonkey2000 and Shareaza (Shareaza also supports BitTorrent).

And yes, if you edit the files, you have to set the sound specification again before saving. ;)

cooked:
Noticed another sound error. Some sentences in the first part of the prophecy are put in the wrong order.
The german text is ordered this way (which is equal to the ordering in the English version):

In the beginning, the 2 mother planets, Zeer (the sun) and Lich (the cold) met one another during the Oogh: the great cataclysm. From this Zeelich was born. Life appeared and the brotherhood of the Gods sent there the youngest son of the light, who was called Kard Nkom. Alas, some deleterious fumes emanating from the Great Sulfur Mine disfigured him. His horribly mutilated face escaped the Gods, and it was decided that Kard Nkom would have to forever hide his face and never again appear in front of the noble assembly. Condemned to roam the cosmos forever, Kard Nkom saw his Name erased from the divine law. He who wanders without a face became then known as Dark Monk Zeelich was populated with 4 different races, condemned by the Gods to fight each other forever. But, life being stronger, the divine brotherhood hurled Zeelich into the first sun of the Novae system. This shock created the planet such as we know it: a blanket of clouds isolating the rocky summits from the melting ground.

But the sentence structurer in the German speech file is like this:

Life appeared and the brotherhood of the Gods sent there the youngest son of the light, who was called Kard Nkom. Alas, some deleterious fumes emanating from the Great Sulfur Mine disfigured him. In the beginning, the 2 mother planets, Zeer (the sun) and Lich (the cold) met one another during the Oogh: the great cataclysm. From this Zeelich was born. His horribly mutilated face escaped the Gods, and it was decided that Kard Nkom would have to forever hide his face and never again appear in front of the noble assembly. Condemned to roam the cosmos forever, Kard Nkom saw his Name erased from the divine law. He who wanders without a face became then known as Dark Monk Zeelich was populated with 4 different races, condemned by the Gods to fight each other forever. But, life being stronger, the divine brotherhood hurled Zeelich into the first sun of the Novae system. This shock created the planet such as we know it: a blanket of clouds isolating the rocky summits from the melting ground.

So thats why I never really understood the prophecy! It actually is complete gibberish!
raw:
Noticed another sound error. Some sentences in the first part of the prophecy are put in the wrong order.
The german text is ordered this way (which is equal to the ordering in the English version):
[quote]
In the beginning, the 2 mother planets, Zeer (the sun) and Lich (the cold) met one another during the Oogh: the great cataclysm. From this Zeelich was born. Life appeared and the brotherhood of the Gods sent there the youngest son of the light, who was called Kard N'kom. Alas, some deleterious fumes emanating from the Great Sulfur Mine disfigured him. His horribly mutilated face escaped the Gods, and it was decided that Kard N'kom would have to forever hide his face and never again appear in front of the noble assembly. Condemned to roam the cosmos forever, Kard N'kom saw his Name erased from the divine law. He who wanders without a face became then known as Dark Monk... Zeelich was populated with 4 different races, condemned by the Gods to fight each other forever. But, life being stronger, the divine brotherhood hurled Zeelich into the first sun of the Novae system. This shock created the planet such as we know it: a blanket of clouds isolating the rocky summits from the melting ground.
[/quote]
But the sentence structurer in the German speech file is like this:
[quote]
Life appeared and the brotherhood of the Gods sent there the youngest son of the light, who was called Kard N'kom. Alas, some deleterious fumes emanating from the Great Sulfur Mine disfigured him. In the beginning, the 2 mother planets, Zeer (the sun) and Lich (the cold) met one another during the Oogh: the great cataclysm. From this Zeelich was born. His horribly mutilated face escaped the Gods, and it was decided that Kard N'kom would have to forever hide his face and never again appear in front of the noble assembly. Condemned to roam the cosmos forever, Kard N'kom saw his Name erased from the divine law. He who wanders without a face became then known as Dark Monk... Zeelich was populated with 4 different races, condemned by the Gods to fight each other forever. But, life being stronger, the divine brotherhood hurled Zeelich into the first sun of the Novae system. This shock created the planet such as we know it: a blanket of clouds isolating the rocky summits from the melting ground.
[/quote]
So that's why I never really understood the prophecy! It actually [i]is[/i] complete gibberish! :p

cooked:
Necro post of necro necromancy!

I still need to fix this 7 years later.

In short, there are a bunch of mistakenly placed soundbytes in the German voice files for LBA 2 (as I explained in detail in my first post.) I can find the voice files by using LBA Text Editor to find the ID and LBA Package Editor to find the appropriate entry in the Voice package, so I could edit this together, finally. The question remains where the totally missing soundbyte about the protection spell has gone to, maybe to a quote never actually heard in the game.

One question to modding folk: What do I need to do with the WAV file after cutting it together in Audacity, so that LBA 2 will accept it? Is there a way to use the compression algorithm Battler spoke?
raw:
Necro post of necro necromancy!

I still need to fix this 7 years later. :p

In short, there are a bunch of mistakenly placed soundbytes in the German voice files for LBA 2 (as I explained in detail in my first post.) I can find the voice files by using LBA Text Editor to find the ID and LBA Package Editor to find the appropriate entry in the Voice package, so I could edit this together, finally. The question remains where the totally missing soundbyte about the protection spell has gone to, maybe to a quote never actually heard in the game.

One question to modding folk: What do I need to do with the WAV file after cutting it together in Audacity, so that LBA 2 will accept it? Is there a way to use the compression algorithm Battler spoke?

cooked: With Audacity, no idea. However, I know Adobe Audition supports IMA ADPCM .WAV files, so you should look into that.
